info.name: Edge Agent B3 — Streaming + Capacity Contention
info.description:
Tests the UX contract between the scheduler and both MFEs (Meeting Summariser + Document Q&A) when the edge agent is at 4/4 capacity with active SSE streams. Covers slot allocation, queue visibility, cancellation, retry, and recovery sequences.

Setup: baseUrl=http://127.0.0.1:8741, apiKey=ek_live_your_key_here

item.name: 1. Contention Setup (4/4 Slots)
item.description: Fill all 4 edge agent slots before Doc Q&A arrives. Meeting Summariser holds slots 0+1 as long-running P0 SSE streams. Slots 2+3 occupied by batch NMT requests.

request.description:
Opens SSE stream for live TTS audio output. Holds slot 1 alongside ASR.

Expected: 200 with Content-Type: text/event-stream. Slot 1 occupied.
Meeting Summariser per-MFE count: 2/2 (at quota).

body.raw:
{
  "model": "nmt-v3",
  "input": "Translate this document from Spanish to English",
  "stream": false,
  "caller_id": "batch-worker",
  "request_id": "req_nmt_001"
}

request.description:
Represents two batch NMT requests filling slots 2 and 3. Run this request twice (request_id: req_nmt_001, req_nmt_002) to occupy both slots.

Expected after 2 calls: 200 for each. Global slot count: 4/4. Doc Q&A will now be queued.

item.name: 2. Doc Q&A Queued (P1 at SATURATED)
item.description: Doc Q&A submits a normal-priority inference request while all 4 slots are occupied. Scheduler enqueues it in P1 lane and emits scheduler:queued event. No 429 — queueing is correct behavior.

item.name: 2.1 Doc Q&A Inference — P1 Hits Queue

body.raw:
{
  "model": "llm-v2",
  "input": "What are the termination clauses in Section 4?",
  "stream": false,
  "caller_id": "doc-qa",
  "request_id": "req_docqa_001"
}

request.description:
Submits doc Q&A query at normal (P1) priority. All 4 slots are taken — scheduler enqueues.

Expected: 202 Accepted (queued, not rejected).
Event emitted: scheduler:queued { position: 1, estimated_wait_ms: ~500 }
Doc Q&A quota: 0/2 in-flight, slots occupied by others.

item.name: 2.2 Subscribe to Scheduler Events — Queued Position

request.description:
SSE subscription to receive scheduler events for doc-qa's request. MFEs see only their own queue state — not other MFEs' positions.

Expected events:
  data: {"event":"scheduler:queued","request_id":"req_docqa_001","position":1,"estimated_wait_ms":500}

Doc Q&A UI should render: 'Queued — position 1 of queue' with indeterminate progress bar.

item.name: 3. Slot Freed — Doc Q&A Dispatched
item.description: When a batch NMT slot completes, the scheduler's dispatch loop fires immediately. Doc Q&A dequeues from P1 lane and is dispatched with queued_ms recorded.

item.name: 3.1 Batch Slot Completes — Check Slot State

request.description:
Receives scheduler:dispatched event after slot freed. Includes queued_ms showing how long Doc Q&A waited.

Expected event:
  data: {"event":"scheduler:dispatched","request_id":"req_docqa_001","queued_ms":200,"slot":2}

Doc Q&A UI transitions: 'Queued — position 1' → 'Processing your question...' (determinate spinner).

item.name: 4. Cancellation Scenarios
item.description: Three cancellation scenarios during contention. Each scenario tests slot accounting and cross-MFE independence.

item.name: 4.1 Scenario A — Cancel Queued Doc Q&A (no slot freed)

request.description:
Doc Q&A unmounts while still in the P1 queue (never dispatched). Scheduler removes QueueEntry. No slot was ever consumed — nothing freed.

Expected: 200 OK
Event: scheduler:cancelled { was_queued: true, request_id: '{{requestId}}' }
Slot count: unchanged (4/4 still)
Meeting Summariser: completely unaffected.

item.name: 4.2 Scenario B — Cancel In-flight Doc Q&A (slot freed)

request.description:
Doc Q&A cancels after being dispatched (in-flight). AbortController fires → fetch() aborted → TCP RST → agent drops request → slot freed.

Expected: 200 OK
AbortController chain: MFE signal → Scheduler AC → fetch() AC → TCP RST
Slot count: drops from 4/4 to 3/4
Dispatch loop fires: next queued item (if any) dequeues within <0.15ms.

item.name: 4.3 Scenario C — Meeting Summariser Stops (2 slots freed, doc-qa dispatches)

request.description:
User stops recording — Meeting Summariser calls cancelByCaller('meeting-sum'). Both ASR and TTS streams aborted simultaneously. 2 slots freed. Quota recalculates: only 1 MFE active, doc-qa max = 4.

Expected: 200 OK
Slot count: drops from 4/4 to 2/4
Doc Q&A: dequeued and dispatched with actual_queued_ms ~50ms
Event: scheduler:dispatched { slot: 0, queued_ms: 50 }

item.name: 5. Retry Semantics
item.description: MFEs own retry decisions. Scheduler returns retrying: false on all error codes. Each error code has a different recommended retry strategy.

body.raw:
{
  "model": "llm-v2",
  "input": "What are the termination clauses in Section 4?",
  "stream": false,
  "caller_id": "doc-qa",
  "request_id": "req_docqa_002"
}

request.description:
Second attempt after the 30s timeout. MFE shows 'Timed out — Retry?' button. User clicks it; MFE re-submits.

Previous error: { status: 'error', code: 'timeout', retrying: false }
New request: fresh 30s timer starts at new submission.
Expected: 202 (queued if still 4/4) or 200 OK (if slot available).

Subscribe to receive connection:restored event. When agent goes offline and comes back, MFE auto-resubmits its last request without user action.

Expected SSE event: data: {"event":"connection:restored","timestamp":1700000000000}
MFE action on receipt: re-call inference() with original request body.
Only one auto-retry per disconnected error — subsequent failures become user-triggered.

P2 background request preempted by incoming P0. Scheduler re-queues at head of P2 lane — invisible to MFE.

Expected MFE behavior: no change in UI state (still sees 'Processing').
Internal: request moves from DISPATCHED → QUEUED at P2 lane head.
P1 and P0 cannot be preempted. Only P2 is eligible.

item.name: 6. Recovery Sequences
item.description: Three contention-specific failure scenarios. Each tests that the scheduler and MFEs recover to a consistent state without data loss.

request.description:
Poll health endpoint after simulated agent crash. Scheduler transitions to SUSPENDED. All SSE streams fail with stream_interrupted error.

Expected during crash: { status: 'unhealthy', state: 'SUSPENDED' }
Meeting Summariser: AsyncGenerator throws, MFE shows 'Connection lost — buffers partial transcript'
Doc Q&A: if queued, stays queued (timer continues). If in-flight, gets disconnected error.
On recovery: DRAINING state, queued requests dispatch in order.

Submit Doc Q&A while Meeting Summariser holds all slots for > 30s. Timeout applies from submission, not from dispatch.

Timeline:
  t=0: queued (position 1)
  t=20s: age-based promotion P1→P0 (priority escalated at WFQ head)
  t=30s: TIMED_OUT — { status: 'error', code: 'timeout', retrying: false }
  t=30s+: MFE shows 'Timed out — Retry?' button

Note: promotion at t=20s does not create a new slot. 30s is the hard upper bound.

item.name: 6.3 User Stops Recording — Happy Path Dispatch

request.description:
Best-case recovery: user naturally stops the meeting. Meeting Summariser calls cancelByCaller. Both SSE streams close cleanly.

Expected chain:
  1. TCP RST → 2 slots freed
  2. Dispatch loop fires <0.15ms
  3. Doc Q&A dequeued (actual_queued_ms: ~50ms)
  4. scheduler:dispatched event received by doc-qa
  5. Doc Q&A inference begins — answer delivered normally

This is the target steady state: contention resolves when natural stream lifecycle completes.

item.name: 7. SSE Streaming (Direct)
item.description: Direct SSE endpoint validation. Verify stream open, token format, done signal, and connection teardown.

request.description:
Open a live SSE connection directly on a stream endpoint. Validates content-type header and initial connection response.

Expected response headers:
  Content-Type: text/event-stream; charset=utf-8
  Cache-Control: no-cache
  Connection: keep-alive
  X-Accel-Buffering: no

Expected initial event:
  data: {"event":"stream:ready","stream_id":"{{streamId}}"}
